steps.title: Spot the exception that needs explaining
steps.content:
Before reading the choices, identify the logical relationship between the two sentences.

Sentence 1: Scientists could quickly isolate some elements (like cadmium) — but not all of them.
Sentence 2: The blank + "the isolation process took years."

The relationship is frequency/scope: sentence 2 explains how often or under what conditions the exception occurred. It doesn't reverse the contrast (that was already done by "but" in sentence 1) — it specifies what happens when the quick-isolation pattern doesn't apply.
  • However — signals contrast; but sentence 1 already contrasted with "but." A second contrast word would create redundancy and a logical loop.
  • Sometimes — specifies frequency: the isolation took years some of the time, which is exactly the partial scope sentence 2 needs to convey.
  • Thus — signals a result/conclusion, but sentence 2 is not a consequence of sentence 1.
  • Moreover — adds information of equal weight; but sentence 2 is narrowing scope, not adding a parallel point.
That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Select the word that explains the exception
steps.content:
“Sometimes the isolation process took years” specifies what happens when the cadmium pattern doesn't apply—answering what “not all elements” actually means: sometimes the process took a long time.

The other three either replay the contrast already stated, falsely suggest causation, or present information as supplementary rather than explanatory.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
